HediffDef.label: Tachycardia
HediffDef.description: Abnormally rapid heart rate (above 140 BPM). Typically a physiological response to pain, sympathetic compensation for blood loss, or adrenaline injection.

HediffDef.label: Bradycardia
HediffDef.description: Abnormally slow heart rate (below 45 BPM). Commonly observed in morphine sedation, terminal hemorrhagic shock, hypothermia, or heart failure.

HediffDef.label: Arrhythmia
HediffDef.description: Irregular heart rhythm. Often caused by cardiac conduction abnormalities due to severe acidosis, electrolyte imbalance, hypoxia, or drug overload.
